incorrect.source_says: This is a multi-organ REVIEW (not a single-protocol paper); the bundle is tagged thyroid and the document covers ~20 tissues. 'Lung' is not the paper's subject.
incorrect.evidence_quote: To date, primary organoids have been derived to a large number of fetal and adult human tissues ( Figure 2A ). ... A list of the culture conditions utilised to culture primary organoids of different human tissues is presented in Table 1 .
incorrect.source_loc: methods_text / section 'Primary organoids derivation and culture'; bundle organoid_type='thyroid'

recall_misses.source_says: Source describes ECM embedding agents and decellularized ECM hydrogels as a core component; matrix.name should at least be 'ECM hydrogel / synthetic gel', not null.
recall_misses.evidence_quote: II) A cytocompatible and protein-rich embedding agents mimicking the tissue’s extracellular matrix (ECM); ... the extracellular matrix (e.g., ECM hydrogels, synthetic gels) is important

notes: PMC10029057 is a REVIEW article (Frontiers Bioeng Biotechnol 2023), not a single derivable protocol. It has no single cell line, matrix %, base medium, or dose — culture conditions live as per-organ factor LISTS in Table 1 (no concentrations). The extractor (tier1_local::gemma3:12b) mis-set organoid_type to 'lung' (bundle tag is 'thyroid'; true answer = multi-organ/none), pulled only ~8 generic narrative factors, and ignored Table 1 entirely — the single richest culture-relevant source. small_molecules, timeline, modifications are empty; matrix is null despite explicit ECM discussion. Two evidence quotes (CHIR, EGF) are duplicated/mis-cited. Of the factors it did extract, the names are individually present in source (correct), so correctness is partial but recall against Table 1 is very low. Concentrations are correctly null because the review reports none. multimodal=true: 3 tables + 2 figures present; recipe lives in tables which Tier-1 text extraction missed (see extraction_warnings: 'tables_present (3) — recipe may live in tables'). n_fields_checked counts the 8 extracted factors + organoid_type + matrix + base_media + 4 high-level slots (small_molecules, timeline, passaging, assay_endpoints) actually compared.
